description:
lib.rs:192 and lib.rs:296 build URLs as format!("{}/v1/{}/data/{}", self.addr, self.mount, self.secret_path). The mount and secret_path strings come from SecretUri::parse() which percent-DECODES the path (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1157). They are then re-interpolated into a URL without percent-ENCODING.

Trigger: a secret_path containing '?', '#', ' ', '%', '+', or any other URL-reserved char produces a malformed URL.
- '?foo' splits into a query string → wrong API call, may 404 or hit unintended path
- '#frag' is dropped client-side → wrong path
- ' ' or '%' produces a 400 Bad Request from reqwest's URL validation

Bad behavior: silent path mismatch on '?' / '#' (returns NotFound/Backend for the wrong reason); client-side reject on ' ' or '%'.

Vault paths legitimately may contain spaces. Threat model in the doc comment says URIs come from admin-controlled config, so this is not a security finding per se — it is a usability/correctness finding: admins encoding a legitimate path with a space cannot use it.

Fix: percent-encode mount and secret_path components when building the URL, or document the restriction explicitly in the URI grammar.
title: URL path components not percent-encoded — special chars in mount/secret_path break request
